Electric Power Consumption & CO₂ Emissions Forecasting

Time Series Analysis | Europe & Central Asia | 1981–2023

Tracy Nguyen

2026-05-08

1 Overview

This report analyses electric power consumption per capita across Europe & Central Asia from 1981 to 2023, and its relationship with CO₂ emissions from waste. Five forecasting models were built and evaluated against the actual 2023 value.

Techniques used: Data wrangling · Missing value imputation · Outlier detection · Stationarity testing (ADF) · Moving Average · ARIMA · Exponential Smoothing · ggplot2

2 Data Cleaning Summary

Missing values in Electric Power Consumption were filled using linear regression on CO₂ Emissions, justified by a pre-imputation correlation of r = 0.716. Outliers were detected using the IQR rule and reviewed before and after imputation.

3 Time Series Analysis

3.1 Trend

ACF analysis found no clear seasonality — the series shows a slow decay indicating a dominant upward trend. Linear regression confirms an increase of approximately 21 kWh per capita per year (R² = 0.685, p < 0.001).

3.2 Residuals (Irregular Component)

Residuals are roughly normally distributed and randomly scattered around zero, suggesting the linear trend model captures the main pattern well.

4 Forecasting Models

Models were trained on 1981–2022 data. The actual 2023 value (5,536 kWh) was used as a hold-out to evaluate forecast accuracy.

4.1 Accuracy Comparison

Model Accuracy Metrics — Training Set (1981–2022)
RMSE MAE MAPE
MA(1) 90.198 66.366 1.293
MA(2) 87.505 59.910 1.169
ARIMA 90.502 67.245 1.312
SES 90.500 67.137 1.310
Holt’s Linear 88.615 60.008 1.168

MA(2) achieved the lowest RMSE and MAE overall. Holt’s Linear Trend was closest to the actual 2023 value.

4.2 Forecast Plots

5 Relationship: Electricity vs CO₂ Emissions

Correlation coefficient after imputation: r = 0.829 — a strong positive relationship confirming that electricity consumption is a reliable proxy for CO2 emissions in this region.

6 Key Findings

Finding
📈 Trend ~21 kWh per capita increase per year (R² = 0.685)
🏆 Best overall model MA(2) — lowest RMSE and MAE
🎯 Best short-term model Holt’s Linear — predicted 5,522 vs actual 5,536 in 2023
🔗 Correlation r = 0.829 between electricity and CO₂ emissions
🔮 2024 forecast ~5,537–5,540 kWh per capita (continued growth)

Analysis by Tracy Nguyen | ANL557 Applied Forecasting | SUSS 2025
